edge_hypothesis: KAMA and CCI are independently validated in separate ACCEPT-tier strategies but have never been combined. KAMA Stoch v1 (PPR=4.268, PF=1.857) proved KAMA's adaptive speed captures trend regardless of regime. Supertrend CCI v4 (PF=1.290, ACCEPT) proved CCI confirms momentum direction — not as an oscillator extreme fade, but as a zero-cross momentum recovery signal. This spec fuses both: KAMA > EMA_50 defines the adaptive trend, CCI crossing above 0 signals momentum has recovered from a pullback. The thesis is that KAMA filters out noise during ranging/transitional regimes (its proven strength) while CCI zero-cross provides a higher-quality re-entry signal than Stochastic (which depends on fixed overbought/oversold levels that shift across regimes). Invalidation: (1) if trade count < 30, the dual filter is too restrictive — relax to CCI > -50; (2) if PF < 1.0, the CCI zero-cross timing adds no edge over random re-entry within a KAMA trend; (3) if ranging PF drops vs KAMA Stoch, Stochastic is the superior confirmation for KAMA.

variants.name: cci_kama_momentum_recovery_8to1
variants.description: KAMA adaptive trend + CCI zero-cross momentum recovery on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R. KAMA > EMA_50 confirms trend direction with adaptive lag. CCI crosses above 0 signals momentum has recovered from a pullback dip. The 8:1 R:R is the proven sweet spot across all ACCEPT strategies. This tests whether CCI zero-cross provides better re-entry timing than Stochastic within a KAMA-defined trend.
entry_long: KAMA_10_2_30 > EMA_50 (adaptive moving average above medium-term EMA — confirmed uptrend with adaptive lag).
CCI_20_0.015 crosses_above 0 (momentum recovery from pullback — CCI returning to positive territory).
entry_short: KAMA_10_2_30 < EMA_50 (adaptive moving average below medium-term EMA — confirmed downtrend).
CCI_20_0.015 crosses_below 0 (momentum collapse — CCI turning negative).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, T3, KAMA, ALMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels, QQE, Choppiness Index, Vortex, STC, Stiffness.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11% win rate to break even after fees.
Fuses two ACCEPT-tier families: KAMA baseline (PPR=4.268) + CCI confirmation (Supertrend CCI v4 ACCEPT).
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: KAMA_10_2_30 > EMA_50 — adaptive trend detection, proven PPR=4.268 family
CCI_20 crosses_above 0 — momentum recovery from pullback, proven in Supertrend CCI v4 ACCEPT
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 8.0x TP — 8:1 proven sweet spot

variants.name: cci_kama_momentum_recovery_10to1
variants.description: KAMA + CCI on ETH/4h with 10:1 R:R. Tests whether the CCI zero-cross signal captures continuation moves large enough for 10:1 payoff. KAMA Stoch topped out at 8:1 — does CCI's momentum-based timing allow larger exits? At 9% breakeven win rate, even low-frequency signals can profit.

entry_short: KAMA_10_2_30 < EMA_50 (adaptive moving average below medium-term EMA — confirmed downtrend).

variants.name: cci_kama_momentum_recovery_6to1
variants.description: KAMA + CCI on ETH/4h with 6:1 R:R. Lower R:R control variant — tests whether the signal quality is strong enough for higher-frequency wins at a shorter TP. If PF peaks here rather than at 8:1, CCI zero-cross captures shorter moves than Stochastic pullbacks, and the optimal R:R for this family is lower.
